summary: Re-tested LandingTourVideo fix. INFINITE-LOOP IS FIXED — exactly 11 'clip failed to load' warnings (one per clip), networkidle in ~5s, no retry storm. BUT a new critical bug surfaced: when poster fallback is active, clicking the PLAY overlay does NOT start the audio narration, breaking the documented graceful-degradation PASS path.

ui_bugs.issue: When all clips fail and the poster fallback renders, videoRef.current is null. The guard `if (!a || !v) return;` short-circuits BEFORE `a.play()`, so the narration audio never starts. The play overlay also never disappears (hasStarted stays false). Verified: after clicking [data-testid='landing-tour-play-overlay'] twice, audio_paused=true, audio_currentTime=0, overlay still visible.

integration_issues.issue: All 11 customer-assets MP4s emit 'clip failed to load' on this preview env (headless Chromium codec set). This is the previously-documented headless codec limitation and matches the brief — fallback path is intended. However the fallback's audio gating is broken (see above).

issues: Play overlay sits on top of the poster fallback; the kicker text 'Own the network · Feel the vibez' is partly hidden behind the big play button (z-index stacking). Cosmetic but worth tightening once the audio-gating bug is fixed.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): In /app/frontend/src/components/landing/LandingTourVideo.tsx — change the early-return in start()/togglePlay()/restart() from `if (!a || !v) return;` to `if (!a) return;` and wrap every `v.play()`/`v.pause()`/`v.load()`/`v.currentTime = …` with `if (v) {...}` so that the audio still plays when only the poster fallback is mounted. Also flip setHasStarted(true) regardless of v presence so the play overlay dismisses.

OPTIONAL: prefetch the first clip with a HEAD/range fetch and only set `allClipsFailed` after the user clicks play, so the section doesn't pre-degrade to the fallback before the user has interacted (the current behaviour is functional but reveals 'codec not supported' early).
OPTIONAL: tighten the play-overlay z-order over the fallback so the headline 'Own the network · Feel the vibez' isn't bisected by the play button.
critical_code_review_comments: The retry-cap fix is correct and works: failedClipsRef + allClipsFailed cleanly bound the onError chain to N=11 warnings then stop.
Removing crossOrigin='anonymous' is correct (no canvas decode happens here).

However the gating of audio playback on `v` being non-null is a regression of the graceful-degradation contract. The audio element is intentionally rendered outside the ternary, but start()/togglePlay()/restart() each force the video to exist before they will touch the audio.

success_rate.frontend: infinite-loop fix: PASS; graceful-degradation audio: FAIL

test_credentials: betatester1@globalvibez.com / BetaTester2026! (not exercised this run — task is scoped to landing video re-test; /beta-hub and /dashboard items deferred until the critical audio-gating bug is fixed)
context_for_next_testing_agent: After the start()/togglePlay()/restart() guard is loosened, re-run: (1) click [data-testid='landing-tour-play-overlay'] on / and assert audio.paused===false within 3s even when poster-fallback is rendered; (2) confirm warning count stays ≤11; (3) then exercise /beta-hub (login betatester1@globalvibez.com / BetaTester2026!) and verify the 9-card grid + at-least-5 status='ok' pings, and /dashboard banner [data-testid='dashboard-beta-hub-banner'] navigates to /beta-hub.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE — In LandingTourVideo.tsx lines 275-284 (start), 286-300 (togglePlay), 310-321 (restart), the first executable line is `if (!a || !v) return;`. When `allClipsFailed === true`, the `<video data-testid='landing-tour-video-clip'>` element is replaced by the `<div data-testid='landing-tour-poster-fallback'>`, so videoRef.current === null. The early-return therefore aborts before `a.play()` is called, leaving the narration silent and the play-overlay visible. REPRODUCTION: open / in headless Chromium (or any browser where all 11 MP4s fail), wait for fallback to mount, click the overlay — audio stays paused, overlay stays. MITIGATION: replace the conjunctive guard with `if (!a) return;` and wrap every video-ref dereference with a null-check; also call `setHasStarted(true)` regardless of `v` so the overlay dismisses in the fallback branch.
